channel.title: Comments for page "The Foundation never changes"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "The Foundation never changes"

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: I know I'm not supposed to reply to these but if someone wants to correct this to american spellings, I don't actually mind on this tale as Fallout is very american. As I'm British it won't be immediately obvious when I'm doing a British spelling so if anyone wants to correct the british spellings to american spellings, I'm fine with that :)

item.title: Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Just noting that a recent change was reverted, as it included spelling fixes that were unnecessary.

For the record, UK-variant spellings (e.g. realise, civilisation) are allowed on the site. If someone has a serious issue with those spellings, contact the author about them first. It's a stylistic decision.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The punctuation issues make it hard to read. I say this after only having read the introduction (which was great [except for the punctuation]). This really has to be good for me to excuse the punctuation problems.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I guess Jeremy is meant to sound a bit kind of innocent cause he doesn't know any better but they're more sort of late 20's. In my mind I was thinking of Nuka Break when coming up with the characters.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Freaking Vault-tec….upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tbh it's my fault, I thought I grammar checked it but I done goofed :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do, but people look up to you. You tell them to stick periods in there, they will. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They seemed like children to me in their personality. Whoops. I should read closer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You get what I mean dammit

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Children? They're not children? I don't understand., fixed the grammar errors though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Vault 25" they both said in unison.

Missing period.

"Well technically no, however there might be some supplies in here that we'll need" Lucy said as they walked into the Vault.

Missing period.

"Well that's creepy" Lucy said as she carried onto the next door.

Missing period.

Actually, he's missing commas there, not periods.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Vault 25" they both said in unison.

Missing period.

"Because I've just found Vault 25"

Missing period.

"Well technically no, however there might be some supplies in here that we'll need" Lucy said as they walked into the Vault.

Missing period.

"Well that's creepy" Lucy said as she carried onto the next door.

Missing period.

This is why there are currently two sentry guns pointed at your heads"

Missing period.

"I was known as O5-6. My real name, is something that was lost a long time ago"

Missing period.

Jeremy asked Lucy, not wishing to go against what they had agreed with O5-6

Missing period.

For this, and that I feel the prose is weak (the children don't seem like children, their personalities are not relatable to me, some sentences are badly worded, there is a lot of telling and not showing), I'm downvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice crossover! I've have always enjoyed these crossover works and this one just hit second place for me!

(maybe Scooby Doo will be the next PC….)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
THANK YOU SO MUCH FOR COMBINING MY TWO FAVORITE FANDOMS.

I had to capitalize that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah that's fair enough, it's difficult to make these accessible to everyone, thank you for reading it anyway though :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Effing good."

-All I have to say

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not generally a fan of Project Crossover stuff, since most of the time it involves things that I have no experience with and thus I have absolutely no idea what's going on in the tale. This sort of falls under that category.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah it's more as if the vault has fallen into disrepair somewhat like Aperture Science has at the beginning of Portal 2. I could possibly add in some hacking parts and I can understand that part but I think it was more of a shock on their part and that they likely will talk about it again (hence the eyebot)

O5-6 knowing about the outside world is more due to her being in a stasis like thing (Like Mr House) and being in control of eyebots used for scouting the wasteland and in my mind, the Foundation would still be able to gain access to feeds from robots like the securitrons.

Edit: Made some edits, nothing too major but hopefully it makes a little bit more sense now.

This is stuff I would explain into the tale but I found it difficult to find a place for them to fit. I will edit some parts to make more sense though :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea of the Foundation building their own Vaults. And I know that the ease of access is in keeping with the games, but I just don't see the Foundation letting it be that easy. There are some weak spots, particularly the end with them just… accepting it and going straight back to what they were doing before finding the Vault. I'm also curious as to how O5-6 knows so much about the outside world, considering that the Vault was (theoretically) closed off from the outside world.

Still, nice use of Teddy, there.

item.title: O lawd
item.content:encoded:
So this is my Fallout project crossover tale. Thank you to Blackwing and Echo for helping with this in chat and anyone else who has helped (who I may have forgotten)

If there's any grammar errors or spelling mistakes you see, feel free to change them.
